mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates expanding consumer demand which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This signal is continuously active as consumer spending is a persistent driver of economic growth.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 367670 in Sep 2010 shows robust consumer spending
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month declines are not followed by negative returns (rolling 12-month hit rate < 40%) or if the signal's average forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate profits, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This threshold is based on historical recessionary levels.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage 9.6% in Sep 2010 exceeds 8% threshold
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 8%, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or if the negative return skew is reversed over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad based economic expansion from both manufacturing and construction sectors. When both INDPRO and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, it signals sustainable growth that supports equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 93.21 in Aug 2010, HOUST 598 in Aug 2010
failure_condition: If the joint signal of positive growth for both proxies yields a 60-day forward return median <= 0 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), equity markets anticipate eventual monetary easing and fiscal stimulus, which may lead to positive forward returns. However, this hypothesis posits that persistently high unemployment is a negative signal for earnings, thus predicting negative returns. In this regime, negative SPX returns are expected.
regime_hint: UNRATE > 8% sustained for at least 3 months
failure_condition: If within the regime of UNRATE > 8%, the average forward 20-day return is not negative and does not differ from non-regime periods at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects increased output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and, with a lag, equity valuations. This positive trend signal is continuously active as industrial output is a core economic growth driver.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO index at 93.21 in Aug 2010 reflecting moderate growth
failure_condition: If the weekly (5-day) forward returns following positive INDPRO readings have a mean return <= 0 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the Consumer Price Index (more than 0.3% month-over-month) signals rising inflation that prompts market expectations of monetary tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If following a CPI monthly increase >0.3%, the average 5-day SPX return is positive, or if the negative signal rate is below 55% over testing period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a virtuous cycle of employment income leading to consumption, which is a powerful driver of corporate earnings. This joint signal is particularly predictive of positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint positive signal does not produce a median 60-day forward return > 0.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (ZLB), conventional monetary policy is exhausted, and further easing must come from unconventional tools like QE. This regime is associated with positive equity returns as markets anticipate liquidity support and portfolio rebalancing.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% in Sep 2010, effectively zero
regime_hint: zero lower bound regime with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
failure_condition: If during ZLB periods the forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test against zero) or if the signal outperforms non-ZLB periods.
